steps.title: Identify the noun and what follows it
steps.content: The word fungus names the organism. The scientific name Hydnum albidum immediately follows to identify which fungus. After that comes a comma-enclosed phrase, commonly referred to as white hedgehog, that adds extra information.

steps.title: Apply punctuation for identifying names
steps.content:
When a specific name directly identifies which noun you mean, no punctuation separates them—the scientific name Hydnum albidum identifies which fungus, so no punctuation should follow fungus.

A period ends the sentence early, a colon introduces lists rather than identifying names, and a comma over-punctuates this identifying relationship.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
